mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicate expanding labor demand, which boosts household income and corporate earnings, driving S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows negative growth in at least 30% of monthly observations over a 2-year rolling window and SPX forward 20-day returns are not positively correlated (Rank IC < 0.02), reject.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in housing starts (HOUST) above the 900,000 unit threshold signals strengthening residential investment and associated consumer spending, which is positive for equity markets over the following month.
failure_condition: If HOUST > 900 occurs but SPX forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances over a 3-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously low levels of consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL growth below 2% YoY) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS below 1%) create accommodative financial conditions that compress discount rates and boost equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
regime_hint: low inflation and low interest rate environment
failure_condition: If the joint condition holds but SPX forward 60-day returns are negative more than 40% of the time over a 5-year walk-forward, or the correlation between the composite signal and returns is zero, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is elevated above 6%, rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates that the manufacturing sector is leading a recovery, which boosts investor sentiment and forward equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If under the trigger condition SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of occurrences, or if the rank correlation between INDPRO growth and returns in high-unemployment periods is below 0.05, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in real retail and food services sales (RSAFS) reflect robust consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If RSAFS month-over-month growth is positive but SPX forward 5-day returns are not also positive at a statistically significant rate (t-test p < 0.05 over 5-year window), reject.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below the 98 index level signals a contraction in manufacturing activity, which reduces corporate profitability and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If INDPRO < 98 occurs but SPX forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such events over a 3-year period, or if the median return under trigger is not negative, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and a decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signal a strengthening labor market that boosts aggregate demand and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.2% and UNRATE month-over-month decline > 0.1%
failure_condition: If the joint signal triggers but the average SPX forward 20-day return is not positive over a 4-year period, or the Fisher combination of rank correlations is insignificant, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year growth in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) is below 2%, the Federal Reserve maintains an accommodative stance with a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS), which supports equity valuations by lowering discount rates and boosting risk appetite.
failure_condition: If under this regime condition the SPX forward 20-day returns are not positive with a Sharpe ratio above 0.5 over a 5-year rolling period, reject.
